message:
fix(json,datetime): handle escaped brackets in JSON path parsing, improve integer/float JSON conversion, and fix fractional month/year modifier fallback

JSON extension (ext-json/lib.rs):
- Fix parse_path() to handle backslash-escaped characters inside bracket
  notation (e.g. $.["key\]with\]brackets"]), preventing premature bracket
  termination that would corrupt path segment extraction
- Improve sqlite_to_json() integer conversion to attempt f64 conversion
  first for large integers, matching SQLite's behavior of representing
  integers that exceed JSON number precision as floating-point
- Optimize merge_patch() to use in-place mutation via get_mut()/mem::replace
  instead of remove-then-reinsert, avoiding unnecessary hash map rehashing
- Update jsonb_set test to use value 9 instead of 2 for clearer test output

DateTime functions (func/datetime.rs):
- Fix apply_month_year_exact() to return Err(()) instead of None when
  the modifier value has a fractional component (e.g. "+1.5 months"),
  allowing the caller to fall back to the approximate apply_modifier()
  path which handles fractional values correctly. Previously, fractional
  month/year modifiers would silently return None (treated as invalid)
  instead of falling through to approximate arithmetic.
- Use three-valued return type Result<Option<f64>, ()> to distinguish
  "not applicable, try fallback" (Err) from "overflow" (Ok(None)) from
  "success" (Ok(Some(jdn)))

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
